ogTitle: Seven Hills vs Seventeen Mile: Climate Safety

heading: Seven Hills vs Seventeen Mile — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Seven Hills, Queensland and Seventeen Mile, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Seven Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Seventeen Mile (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Seven Hills or Seventeen Mile safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Seven Hills has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Seventeen Mile scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Seven Hills scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Seventeen Mile scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Seven Hills or Seventeen Mile?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Seven Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
